authorization: Authorize S12-D1 fresh-candidate Tier-N spec draft review only.

candidate_summary.data_reuse_strategy: REUSES existing audit-clean MNQ.c.0 CSV from s10-D1 byte-equivalent (sha 8b7b832c62fae1854fbf664db9c79ec953d4462ff6d89896cc39975005dfa23e)
candidate_summary.mechanic_family: F1 -- Long+Short Bi-Directional Donchian Trend, No Pyramid, ATR-based Stop, Single MNQ.c.0 Contract Per Signal

C1_oos_k9_handling.issue: DRAFT addresses IS K9 explicitly but is silent on OOS K9 expectations. By proportional scaling of expected IS trade counts (80-200 over 4.6y), OOS (2y) expected trade counts are 35-87 across all three estimate bounds, ALL BELOW the K9 threshold of 100. This is the same binding constraint that parked s10-D2 (OOS closed_trades=53 below K9=100).
C1_oos_k9_handling.note: Option C1.A is the most disciplined: accept that OOS K9 may still fire, treat it as the structural test of the fresh-candidate hypothesis. Option C1.D affirms this maps to DR1 INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD (not REJECT_FAST), preserving the candidate's IS evidence even if OOS sample under-fires.

options_for_operator_at_seal.detail: Pre-acknowledge that OOS K9 may still fire; if it does, candidate parks under PARKED_SAFE_BUT_OOS_INDETERMINATE_K9_FIRED but with a cleaner cost-stress + trade-density story. This is the most disciplined option.
options_for_operator_at_seal.label: Accept OOS K9 risk as part of structural test

options_for_operator_at_seal.detail: Push OOS end date past 2025-12-30 (additional ~5 months currently available; would require fresh Databento fetch authorization). Adds ~5 months / 24 months = ~21% additional history. Would push central OOS estimate from ~61 to ~74; still below K9=100.
options_for_operator_at_seal.label: Extend OOS window

options_for_operator_at_seal.detail: Add a second symbol to s12-d1's universe to roughly double trade density. Conflicts with single_instrument_universe_NO_widening_post_seal invariant; requires fresh candidate_record_id.
options_for_operator_at_seal.label: Pivot to multi-symbol Donchian-15/8

options_for_operator_at_seal.detail: Already implied by DRAFT section 8.2 DR1 (OOS-only). Affirm at SEAL that OOS K9 sub-threshold maps to DR1 not REJECT_FAST.
options_for_operator_at_seal.label: Carry forward K9 sub-threshold as DR1 INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD on OOS

C2_dr10_mitigation_lever_DA4.issue: DR10 turnover-cost-explosion has ELEVATED prior probability under Donchian-15/8. Mitigation lever DA4=B raises START_CASH from $50k to $100k, reducing per-dollar commission/slip pressure by halving contracts-per-trade.
options_for_operator_at_seal: DA4=A: carry $50k byte-equivalent from s11-d1 v1 (preserves closest baseline)
DA4=B: raise to $100k (mitigates DR10 risk; was s11-d1 DRAFT pre-SEAL default)
DA4=C: lower to $25k (more aggressive; not recommended)
C2_dr10_mitigation_lever_DA4.recommended_at_review_time: DA4=B (operator decides at SEAL turn; the elevated DR10 risk is real)

C3_da_register_completeness.issue: DA register covers 14 items. DA1-DA5 are genuinely negotiable; DA6-DA14 are framework-locked. The structure is clean.
C3_da_register_completeness.note: No additional clarifications needed for the DA register itself.

C4_post_oos_data_use.issue: DRAFT section 7 marks post-OOS data (2026-01-02 onward) as informational only.
C4_post_oos_data_use.note: Confirm at SEAL that post-OOS data cannot be re-promoted to OOS scope. Already locked by oos_inspection_blocked_at_in_sample invariant and the structural OOS window.

draft_under_review.commit_subject: Add s12 D1 MNQ.c.0 Donchian-15/8 fresh-candidate Tier-N spec DRAFT (mechanic family + Donchian 15/8 LOCKED at PLAN)
draft_under_review.draft_status: DRAFT not SEALED; 14 DA items pending operator confirmation at SEAL turn
draft_under_review.predecessor_plan_commit: b4eac65 (s12-d1 PLAN with mechanic family + Donchian-15/8 LOCKED)

seal_method: sha256 over json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) EXCLUDING report_seal_sha256 + seal_method

task_3_k9_mitigation_assessment.draft_admission_verbatim_section_10: K9 risk: borderline at lower bound -- lower estimate 80 < K9; central estimate ~140 clears; upper estimate 200 clears with margin
task_3_k9_mitigation_assessment.is_k9_addressing_method: Donchian-15/8 lookback shortening; expected 80-200 IS trades vs s11-d1 v1's ~25-50
task_3_k9_mitigation_assessment.k9_oos_risk_assessment: NOT_FULLY_MITIGATED: The DRAFT addresses IS K9 directly but is silent on OOS K9 expectations. By proportional scaling, all three IS estimate bounds yield OOS trade counts BELOW the K9 threshold of 100. This means the same binding constraint that parked s10-D2 may re-fire on s12-d1's OOS verdict.

oos_k9_proportional_scaling_check.implication: At all three IS trade-density estimates (lower/central/upper), proportional scaling to the 2-year OOS window yields expected OOS trade counts of 35-87, all below the K9 OOS threshold of 100

task_3_k9_mitigation_assessment.problem_that_parked_s10_d2: OOS closed_trades = 53 on 3-year window for 4-market Donchian-55/20 portfolio; below K9 threshold of 100
task_3_k9_mitigation_assessment.s12_d1_mitigation_mechanic: Shorten Donchian lookbacks from 55/20 to 15/8 (~3-4x signal-density increase); single instrument simplifies the per-symbol vs portfolio distinction

first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_b006_001_002: No leverage cap; B006 leverage-cap pathology structurally absent; DR11 not in chain.
first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_s10_d1: Different universe (MNQ.c.0 only vs MNQ + MGC); MGC structurally absent.
first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_s10_d2: Different universe (single MNQ.c.0 vs 4-market portfolio); different Donchian periods.
first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_s11_d1_v1: Different Donchian periods (15/8 vs 55/20). The s11-d1 v1 SEAL section 9 explicitly states that shortening Donchian periods REQUIRES A FRESH candidate_record_id; s12-d1 satisfies this structurally.
first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_s7_d1: No pyramid (s7-D1 used pyramid); different universe.
first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_s9: Different mechanic family (trend Donchian vs RSI-2 mean-reversion); different universe (futures vs ETF-proxy).
first_principles_burden_addressed.vs_t8_etf_proxy_umbrella: Universe is futures; T8 does not apply.

task_5_cost_stress_from_day_one.pre_registered_k12_composite: DR2 + DR3 (S2/S3/S4 degrade >50% or sign flip)

cost_model.note: 5-tier from day 1 with locked thresholds; byte-equivalent to s11-d1 v1.

data_availability.note: Reuses audit-clean MNQ.c.0 CSV from s10-D1 byte-equivalent; sha 8b7b832c62fae185...; 2066 rows; DR9-clean per s10-D1 audit; zero new Databento fetch required.

dr10_turnover_risk.note: DRAFT section 8.2 and 16.2 explicitly flag this as ELEVATED prior probability. Mitigation lever DA4=B (raising START_CASH from $50k to $100k) is presented as alternative at SEAL. Operator decides at SEAL turn.

insufficient_trade_density_rationale.note: IS trade-density rationale is well-articulated (80-200 over 4.6y). OOS trade-density rationale is IMPLICIT and not addressed explicitly. Proportional scaling suggests OOS trade count of 35-87 across all three IS estimate bounds, all below K9 threshold of 100. This is the SAME binding constraint that parked s10-D2.

is_oos_split.note: Cleanly defined: IS 2019-05-13 to 2023-12-29 (~4.6y / 1,443 rows); OOS 2024-01-02 to 2025-12-30 (~2y / 622 rows). Post-OOS data informational only.

thresholds.note: K9=100, K4=50% (DA5=A default), DR9 thresholds (0.95/0.30/5/5), DR10 thresholds (0.50/0.05) all locked at SEAL.

too_similar_to_parked_candidates.note: DRAFT section 2 explicitly addresses first-principles-burden against s11-d1 v1, s10-D2, s10-D1, s9, s7-D1, B006_001, B006_002, T8. Structurally fresh candidate_record_id.

Universe / Dataset / Schema / stype_in / IS-OOS windows / cost-stress / sample-size / K-gates / DR-gates all locked

No live / no Strategy Lab / no review_queue / no brokerage paths (25 sealed invariants)

DRAFT ambiguity register DA1-DA14 well-bounded (DA1-DA5 genuinely negotiable; DA6-DA14 framework-locked)
